Problem Statement: Compute the derivatives of the vector-valued functions.

Question: $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = e^{t} \mathbf{\text{i}} + 2 e^{t} \mathbf{\text{j}} + \mathbf{\text{k}}$$

Question: $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = t^{2} \mathbf{\text{i}} + t e^{-2 t} \mathbf{\text{j}} - 5 e^{-4 t} \mathbf{\text{k}}$$

Question: $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = t \mathbf{\text{i}} + \text{sin} ( 2 t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} + \text{cos} ( 3 t ) \mathbf{\text{k}} ; t = \frac{\pi}{3}$$

Question: $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \text{cos} ( 2 t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + 2 \text{sin} t \mathbf{\text{j}} + t^{2} \mathbf{\text{k}} ; t = \frac{\pi}{2}$$

Question: $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = 6 \mathbf{\text{i}} + \text{cos} ( 3 t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} + 3 \text{sin} ( 4 t ) \mathbf{\text{k}} ,$$ $$0 \le t < 2 \pi$$ . Two views of this curve are presented here:

Question: $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = 3 \text{cos} ( 4 t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + 3 \text{sin} ( 4 t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} + 5 t \mathbf{\text{k}} , 1 \le t \le 2$$

Question: $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = t \mathbf{\text{i}} + 3 t \mathbf{\text{j}} + t^{2} \mathbf{\text{k}}$$

Problem Statement: Find the following.
Question: $$\frac{d}{d t} [ \mathbf{\text{r}} ( t^{2} ) ]$$

Problem Statement: Find the following.
Question: $$\frac{d}{d t} [ t^{2} \cdotp \mathbf{\text{s}} ( t ) ]$$

Problem Statement: Find the following.
Question: $$\frac{d}{d t} [ \mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{\text{s}} ( t ) ]$$
Answer: $$\text{sin} ( t ) + 2 t e^{t} - 4 t^{3} \text{cos} ( t ) + t \text{cos} ( t ) + t^{2} e^{t} + t^{4} \text{sin} ( t )$$

Problem Statement: Find the following.
Question: Compute the first, second, and third derivatives of $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = 3 t \mathbf{\text{i}} + 6 \text{ln} ( t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} + 5 e^{-3 t} \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$

Problem Statement: Find the following.
Question: Find $$\mathbf{\text{r}} \prime ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{\text{r}} \text{″} ( t ) \text{for} \mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = -3 t^{5} \mathbf{\text{i}} + 5 t \mathbf{\text{j}} + 2 t^{2} \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$

Problem Statement: Find the following.
Question: The acceleration function, initial velocity, and initial position of a particle are $$\mathbf{\text{a}} ( t ) = -5 \text{cos} t \mathbf{\text{i}} - 5 \text{sin} t \mathbf{\text{j}} , \mathbf{\text{v}} ( 0 ) = 9 \mathbf{\text{i}} + 2 \mathbf{\text{j}} , \text{and} \mathbf{\text{r}} ( 0 ) = 5 \mathbf{\text{i}} .$$ Find $$\mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) \text{and} \mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) .$$

Problem Statement: Find the following.
Question: The position vector of a particle is $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = 5 \text{sec} ( 2 t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} - 4 \text{tan} ( t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} + 7 t^{2} \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$ Graph the position function and display a view of the graph that illustrates the asymptotic behavior of the function. Find the velocity as t approaches but is not equal to $$\pi \text{/} 4$$ (if it exists).

Problem Statement: Find the following.
Question: Find the velocity and the speed of a particle with the position function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = ( \frac{2 t - 1}{2 t + 1} ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + \text{ln} ( 1 - 4 t^{2} ) \mathbf{\text{j}} .$$ The speed of a particle is the magnitude of the velocity and is represented by $$\text{‖} r^{\prime} ( t ) \text{‖} .$$

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Find the velocity function and show that $$\mathbf{\text{v}} ( t )$$ is always orthogonal to $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) .$$
Answer: $$\mathbf{\text{r}} \prime ( t ) = - b \upomega \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \upomega \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} .$$ To show orthogonality, note that $$\mathbf{\text{r}} \prime ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = 0 .$$

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Show that the speed of the particle is proportional to the angular velocity.

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Evaluate $$\frac{d}{d t} [ \mathbf{\text{u}} ( t ) \times \mathbf{u^{\prime}} ( t ) ]$$ given $$\mathbf{\text{u}} ( t ) = t^{2} \mathbf{\text{i}} - 2 t \mathbf{\text{j}} + \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Find the antiderivative of $$\mathbf{\text{r}} \prime ( t ) = \text{cos} ( 2 t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} - 2 \text{sin} t \mathbf{\text{j}} + \frac{1}{1 + t^{2}} \mathbf{\text{k}}$$ that satisfies the initial condition $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( 0 ) = 3 \mathbf{\text{i}} - 2 \mathbf{\text{j}} + \mathbf{\text{k}} .$$

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Evaluate $$\int_{0}^{3} \text{‖} t \mathbf{\text{i}} + t^{2} \mathbf{\text{j}} \text{‖} d t .$$

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: An object starts from rest at point $$P ( 1 , 2 , 0 )$$ and moves with an acceleration of $$\mathbf{\text{a}} ( t ) = \mathbf{\text{j}} + 2 \mathbf{\text{k}} ,$$ where $$\text{‖} \mathbf{\text{a}} ( t ) \text{‖}$$ is measured in feet per second per second. Find the location of the object after $$t = 2$$ sec.

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Show that if the speed of a particle traveling along a curve represented by a vector-valued function is constant, then the velocity function is always perpendicular to the acceleration function.
Answer: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}\text{‖} \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) \text{‖} & = & k \\ \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) & = & k \\ \frac{d}{d t} ( \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) ) & = & \frac{d}{d t} k = 0 \\ \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{\text{v}} \prime ( t ) + \mathbf{\text{v}} \prime ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) & = & 0 \\ 2 \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{\text{v}} \prime ( t ) & = & 0 \\ \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) \cdotp \mathbf{\text{v}} \prime ( t ) & = & 0 .\end{matrix}\right .$$ The last statement implies that the velocity and acceleration are perpendicular or orthogonal.

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Given $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = t \mathbf{\text{i}} + 3 t \mathbf{\text{j}} + t^{2} \mathbf{\text{k}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{u}} ( t ) = 4 t \mathbf{\text{i}} + t^{2} \mathbf{\text{j}} + t^{3} \mathbf{\text{k}} ,$$ find $$\frac{d}{d t} ( \mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) \times \mathbf{\text{u}} ( t ) ) .$$

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Given $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle t + \text{cos} t , t - \text{sin} t \rangle ,$$ find the velocity and the speed at any time.
Answer: $$\mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) = \langle 1 - \text{sin} t , 1 - \text{cos} t \rangle ,$$ $$\text{speed} = \text{‖} \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) \text{‖} = \sqrt{3 - 2 ( \text{sin} t + \text{cos} t )}$$

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Find the velocity vector for the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle e^{t} , e^{\text{unicode[:-]} t} , 0 \rangle .$$

Question: Find the equation of the tangent line to the curve $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle e^{t} , e^{\text{unicode[:-]} t} , 0 \rangle$$ at $$t = 0 .$$

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Describe and sketch the curve represented by the vector-valued function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle 6 t , 6 t - t^{2} \rangle .$$

Problem Statement: A particle moves on a circular path of radius b according to the function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = b \text{cos} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{i}} + b \text{sin} ( \upomega t ) \mathbf{\text{j}} ,$$ where $$\upomega$$ is the angular velocity, $$d \theta \text{/} d t .$$
Question: Locate the highest point on the curve $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle 6 t , 6 t - t^{2} \rangle$$ and give the value of the function at this point.

Problem Statement: Given the vector-valued function $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle \text{tan} t , \text{sec} t , 0 \rangle$$ (graph is shown here), find the following:
Question: Speed
Answer: $$\text{‖} \mathbf{\text{v}} ( t ) \text{‖} = \sqrt{\text{sec}^{4} t + \text{sec}^{2} t \text{tan}^{2} t} = \sqrt{\text{sec}^{2} t ( \text{sec}^{2} t + \text{tan}^{2} t )}$$

Problem Statement: Given $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = t \mathbf{\text{i}} + 2 \text{sin} t \mathbf{\text{j}} + 2 \text{cos} t \mathbf{\text{k}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{u}} ( t ) = \frac{1}{t} \mathbf{\text{i}} + 2 \text{sin} t \mathbf{\text{j}} + 2 \text{cos} t \mathbf{\text{k}} ,$$ find the following:
Question: $$\frac{d}{d t} ( \mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) \times \mathbf{\text{u}} ( t ) )$$
Answer: $$\langle 0 , 2 \text{sin} t ( t - \frac{1}{t} ) - 2 \text{cos} t ( 1 + \frac{1}{t^{2}} ) , 2 \text{sin} t ( 1 + \frac{1}{t^{2}} ) + 2 \text{cos} t ( t - \frac{2}{t} ) \rangle$$
